analysis_result: NF1 mutation frequency in the mixed atypical NF / benign NF WES cohort (Supplementary Figure 3a oncoprint) = 41% (14/34 samples with non-silent NF1 mutations). If the full 36-sample MOESM4 tracked WES cohort is used as the denominator, the frequency is 14/36 = 38.9% (~39%); if restricted to oncogenic/likely-oncogenic NF1 variants only, it is 12/36 = 33.3% (~33%). The 41% value in the published oncoprint is consistent with a denominator of 34 (i.e., 14/34 = 41.2%), which matches the number of samples actually displayed in the oncoprint (after exclusion of two of the four samples that have no mutations in the MAF).
denominator_used: N = 34 samples (denominator implied by the 41% figure in the Supplementary Figure 3a oncoprint). The 36-sample MOESM4-tracked benign/atypical NF WES cohort (20 Atypical_NF + 16 Neurofibroma; 0 Cutaneous_NF had WES) yields 14/36 = 38.9%. Four samples (Tumor_882, Tumor_2138, Tumor_2178, Tumor_2414) are absent from MAF; excluding all 4 gives 14/32 = 43.8%, and excluding 2 of them gives 14/34 = 41.2%, which matches the 41% value in the oncoprint.
assumptions_made: NF1 mutation frequency counted at the sample level (any non-silent NF1 variant counts a sample as NF1-mutated); Variant_Classification != 'Silent' used as the non-synonymous filter; Tumor_Sample_Barcode in MAF constructed as 'Tumor_' + WES label from MOESM4; all MAF rows already pass FILTER=PASS and TGL_FILTER_VERDICT=PASS; denominator of 34 (implied by the 41% figure) assumed to correspond to the samples actually included in the oncoprint of Supplementary Figure 3a, which excludes 2 of 4 MOESM4-tracked benign/atypical NF samples that had no mutations in MAF; if alternative denominator N=36 or N=32 was used, the frequency would differ (39% or 44% respectively).
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx (clinical data): 108 rows x 15 cols. Columns used: Tumor_ID, Tumor Type, WES, WES label.
   - 41467_2023_38432_MOESM5_ESM.xlsx (WES MAF): 7457 rows x 123 cols covering 52 unique Tumor_Sample_Barcodes. Columns used: Tumor_Sample_Barcode, Hugo_Symbol, Variant_Classification, oncogenic, oncogenic_binary, FILTER, TGL_FILTER_VERDICT.

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - Filter 1: WES == 'X' in MOESM4 -> 54 samples remaining.
   - Filter 2: Tumor Type in {Atypical_NF, Neurofibroma, Cutaneous_NF} -> 36 samples remaining (20 Atypical_NF, 16 Neurofibroma, 0 Cutaneous_NF).
   - Sample barcode constructed as 'Tumor_' + WES label; 32 of these 36 samples have at least one row in MAF (4 are absent: Tumor_882, Tumor_2138, Tumor_2178, Tumor_2414).
   - Final analytic cohort: 36 tracked WES samples; oncoprint denominator implied by the paper figure = 34.

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - NF1 mutated: any MAF row with Hugo_Symbol == 'NF1' and Variant_Classification != 'Silent' (non-synonymous).
   - Variant_Classification values present in cohort rows: Missense_Mutation (most common), Nonsense_Mutation, Frame_Shift_Del/Ins, Splice_Site, In_Frame_Del/Ins, Translation_Start_Site, Nonstop_Mutation, Silent.
   - All rows already have FILTER=PASS and TGL_FILTER_VERDICT=PASS, so no additional filtering was required.
   - oncogenic column: 40 'Likely Oncogenic' + 2 'Oncogenic' across the full MAF; NaN otherwise.

D. COMPUTATION
   - MAF rows for cohort: 4428 (of 7457 total).
   - Non-silent cohort rows: 2987.
   - Non-silent NF1 rows: sum across 14 unique samples.
   - NF1-mutated samples (non-silent): Tumor_990, Tumor_1117, Tumor_1349, Tumor_1729, Tumor_1799, Tumor_1856, Tumor_1874, Tumor_1901, Tumor_2162, Tumor_2401, Tumor_2477, Tumor_2503, Tumor_2525, Tumor_2797 -> 14 samples.
   - With additional oncogenic filter (oncogenic_binary == 'YES'): 12 samples (excludes Tumor_1856 and Tumor_2525).

E. RESULT
   - Oncoprint value (matches paper's 41%): 14/34 = 41.2% (rounded to 41%).
   - Full MOESM4 WES cohort denominator (N=36): 14/36 = 38.9% (rounds to 39%).
   - Oncogenic/Likely-Oncogenic NF1 only, N=36: 12/36 = 33.3% (rounds to 33%).

F. CAVEATS
   - The exact denominator used by the authors in Supplementary Figure 3a is not entirely determinable from the supplementary tables alone; the 41% figure in the oncoprint is consistent with 14/34 = 41.2% but not with 14/36 (full MOESM4 WES cohort) or 14/32 (samples with any MAF entry).
   - Four MOESM4-tracked benign/atypical-NF WES samples (Tumor_882, Tumor_2138, Tumor_2178, Tumor_2414) are absent from the MAF. They may have been included in the oncoprint as zero-mutation samples (yielding the 36- or 34-sample denominator) or excluded entirely (yielding 32).
   - Using non-silent mutation filter gives 14 NF1 samples; adding an oncogenic filter reduces this to 12 samples and changes the frequency; the published oncoprint count matches the non-silent (non-oncogenic-filtered) count.
